AMGN.final_explanation: AMGN's improving Horizon integration margins and imminent MariTide Phase 2 catalyst create favorable asymmetric risk/reward at a normalized ~23x forward P/E.

DIS.final_explanation: Disney's streaming profitability inflection and genuine earnings acceleration have compressed P/E from 70x to 30x, creating the most attractive risk-reward in three prior decision points. The bear case on valuation has been substantially addressed by real earnings growth, though parks softness and still-elevated multiples warrant moderate rather than high conviction.

GS.final_explanation: GS benefits from Fed easing cycle driving capital markets recovery; valuation is reasonable at 14.8x P/E despite +71% rally as earnings grew faster than price.

JNJ.final_explanation: JNJ's talc overhang is materially de-risking via an $8B prepackaged bankruptcy with 83% claimant support, while the underlying business remains solid with ~$88B annualized revenue and 69% gross margins; at a forward adjusted P/E of ~15.2x the stock offers modest upside if the court approves the settlement and the multiple re-rates toward pharma peers.

MRK.final_explanation: MRK's valuation has improved with the 8% pullback (13.3x forward P/E vs 14.5x in July), but pipeline setbacks (favezelimab Phase 3 failure, FDA PD-1 doubts for gastric cancer) and intensifying ivonescimab competition offset the cheaper entry — maintaining a mild positive lean with lower conviction.

MSFT.final_explanation: MSFT offers mild risk-adjusted reward: genuine AI-driven fundamental acceleration and valuation compression from peak improve the setup, but at 36.5x P/E with 2.7% earnings yield the stock still prices in sustained premium growth.

PG.final_explanation: PG is a quality compounder but the market has bid it to historically expensive levels (~29x PE) on decelerating growth, making risk/reward unfavorable at current prices.
